title: Skywalker Technical Maintenance Service
content:
{{Top|leg}}
[[File:Skywalkertech.jpg|thumb|250px|The ostensible employees of Skywalker Technical Maintenance Service stand in a doorway while the energy lathe that they had promised to fix remains unattended.]]
{{Quote|Your equipment will be just like new, sir!|[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] to Skywalker Technical Maintenance Service's first and only customer|<ref name="Han Solo Solution">{{SWRadio|The Han Solo Solution}}</ref>}}
'''Skywalker Technical Maintenance Service''' was a fake company invented by the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] as a cover story as they were trying to evade [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] on the streets of [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. 

The two droids had escaped from their starship, the ''[[Tantive IV/Legends|Tantive IV]],'' in the aftermath of the [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Tatooine]], and Imperial forces had pursued them ever since, seeking the strategically vital [[Death Star plans/Legends|Death Star Plans]] that had been hidden inside R2-D2 in the final minutes of the battle.<ref name="FilmIV">{{Film|IV}}</ref> Following a [[Command Control Instruction]],<ref name="Black Knight">{{SWRadio|Black Knight, White Princess, and Pawns}}</ref> R2-D2 had sought out the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], bringing along C-3PO and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], the Tatooine farm boy who had become the droids' master. In order to bring the plans to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], Kenobi had brought Skywalker and the droids to Mos Eisley to charter a starship that could take them off-world.<ref name="FilmIV" />

As the two humans made arrangements to sell Skywalker's [[X-34 landspeeder/Legends|X-34 landspeeder]], the two droids were left on their own to walk to the spaceport's [[Docking Bay 94/Legends|Docking Bay 94]]. The streets were full of Stormtroopers hunting for them, and they found that the entrance to the docking bay was locked. Artoo-Detoo, ignoring his counterpart's protests, knocked on a nearby door. The proprietor of a shop answered, and Artoo told him that the droids were there to conduct maintenance. See-Threepio translated and then invented the name of the company on the spot.<ref name="Han Solo Solution" />

Threepio told the proprietor that the brand-new company was conducting a publicity campaign. Its employees were ostensibly going door to door offering to repair, recalibrate, and check the systems on any equipment specified for free. To apply more pressure, Threepio said that it was a one-time offer only. The first and only customer of Skywalker Technical Maintenance Service accepted the offer. He directed the two droids to service an [[energy lathe]] that was running out of sync. He left them to work by themselves and left his shop on an errand.<ref name="Han Solo Solution" />

The two droids saw that stormtroopers were still conducting a house-to-house search of the area. They closed and locked the door and waited for the troopers to pass by.<ref name="Han Solo Solution" /> When they opened the door once again, they spotted Luke Skywalker and Obi-Wan Kenobi heading for Docking Bay 94.<ref name="FilmIV" /> Reunited with their new master, the two employees of Skywalker Technical Maintenance Service abandoned the task that they had promised to take care of without even beginning to ameliorate the energy lathe's unsatisfactory condition.<ref name="Han Solo Solution" />

Skywalker Technical Maintenance Service was an extremely small business. Its labor pool consisted entirely of one [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] and one [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]]. In fact, it was so small that its presumed chief executive—[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]—was likely unaware of its existence.
